title: Fix main widget close button honoring the configured action
description: The close button in the main widget visually presses, but the app only closes from the tray menu. When settings specify Exit app, the widget close button should actually exit the app; when configured for Hide to tray, it should hide reliably.
notes:
2026-03-11: Added missing hide_widget_stack backend command, switched widget header buttons to explicit click handlers with error logging, and rebuilt portable exe after verify/cargo check/build:desktop. Pending manual runtime confirmation on Windows that the main close button now honors Exit app and Hide to tray.
2026-03-11: Follow-up pass switched quit_application to app.exit(0), narrowed drag regions away from the entire window roots, and changed closeWidgetWindow to try the Tauri close invoke path before browser fallback. Rebuilt portable exe after verify/cargo check/build:desktop. Awaiting another manual runtime check for the main widget close button.
2026-03-11: Root cause found in src-tauri/permissions/default.toml. The main widget was not permitted to invoke exit_app or hide_widget_stack, so the close button action failed even though the UI click fired. Added the missing permissions and rebuilt after verify/cargo check/build:desktop. Awaiting user runtime confirmation.
2026-03-11: User confirmed at runtime that the main widget close button now works correctly after the permission fix.
close_reason: User confirmed the runtime close behavior is fixed.

title: Resize the main widget window to fit reconnect and error state cards
description: When the reconnect or error state card appears at the bottom of the main widget, the card and button can extend past the lower edge of the transparent window. The widget window height should be recalculated so the full state card remains visible after state transitions and reconnect actions.
notes: 2026-03-11: Root cause also affected auto-height. The widget was not permitted to invoke set_main_window_height, so the reconnect/error card overflow fix could not apply at runtime. Added the missing permission in src-tauri/permissions/default.toml and rebuilt after verify/cargo check/build:desktop. Awaiting user runtime confirmation.

title: Stabilize current widget interaction regressions
description: Track the current set of user-reported regressions before the next implementation pass. This umbrella issue groups the open widget problems that must be handled through beads first.

title: Migrate task tracking from Beads to markdown project-tracking
description: Adopt the markdown project-tracking workflow from lgg/chatgpt-coding-projects-bootstrap, migrate all Beads issue history/current state into markdown files, update project rules/docs, verify completeness, and remove Beads from the repository after successful migration.

title: Restore settings window dragging on empty frame areas
description: The settings window can no longer be dragged from empty areas near the outer frame and bottom padding. Restore dragging on non-interactive empty surfaces without breaking close button or form interactions.
notes: 2026-03-11: Restored drag-region on the settings window shell, removed no-drag from the scroll container itself, and kept the actual settings cards as no-drag so empty frame/bottom areas can drag again without breaking controls. Rebuilt portable exe after verify/cargo check/build:desktop. Awaiting user runtime confirmation.

title: Run a widget regression smoke pass after current stabilization fixes
description: After the current widget regressions are fixed, run a focused portable smoke pass covering the main window close action, reconnect/error state card layout, settings window interaction, and tray interaction so the next test build does not regress immediately.

title: Validate live Companion auth, realtime, commands, and seek against a real YTMDesktop instance
description: Run the implemented integration against a live local YTMDesktop Companion instance and verify the auth round-trip, realtime state updates, playback commands, reconnect behavior, and actual seek behavior. Capture any protocol mismatches or follow-up fixes as linked issues.

title: Keep Connection State and Authorization cards on one row in settings
description: In the API / Connection section, the Connection State and Authorization summary cards should stay side by side on a single row at the current settings window width instead of stacking into separate rows.
close_reason: Connection State and Authorization now render in a single row in settings.

title: Add richer diagnostics and logging around Companion reconnects and runtime state
description: The architecture notes leave room for better diagnostics around Companion reconnect behavior and runtime state transitions. Add practical logging or inspection hooks that help debug connection failures, auth problems, and window/runtime regressions without relying on guesswork.

title: Plan deferred visual refinement pass and alternate widget window modes
description: The docs intentionally defer deeper visual refinement and alternate window modes beyond the current fixed v1 widget. Capture and later implement the next premium UI pass together with expanded window mode options once runtime stability is handled.

title: Re-enable installer packaging after the portable-only test cycle
description: Portable-only builds are the current default for fast testing. Revisit Tauri bundling and restore installer/setup packaging when packaging validation becomes an active goal again.

title: Add future widget size presets and manual resize support
description: Implement the deferred multi-size widget plan so the app can support alternate window presets and eventual manual resizing without breaking the current 256x256 cover-driven layout.

title: Track deferred post-v1 roadmap items
description: Collect the medium-term and deferred work that was intentionally left out of the current portable-first Windows testing cycle so future implementation passes can pick it up explicitly.

title: Expand localization beyond the English-only v1 bundle
description: The app already externalizes user-facing strings into JSON files. Add at least one additional locale and validate that the existing i18n structure scales without UI regressions.

title: Plan future macOS support for window, tray, and startup behavior
description: The codebase is intentionally Windows-first today. Capture the work needed to adapt tray behavior, startup integration, transparent windows, and settings for a future macOS port.
